mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces discount rates on equity cash flows. A 3-month rolling decline in FEDFUNDS produces positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days as investors reprice equity valuations upward in response to lower cost of capital.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.7% on 2018-05-01 reflects post-crisis accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward return in easing periods is not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% signal deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger equity risk-off behavior. When UNRATE crosses above this threshold, S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days turn negative as investors reduce equity exposure in anticipation of demand destruction.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% on 2018-05-01 indicates tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, equity investors increase risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures coordinated demand expansion that single-series signals miss.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 501,971M and HOUST at 1,287K on 2018-04-01 show robust demand
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized consumer and housing demand
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of 20-day forward return sign, or Rank IC of interaction < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal supply-side weakness and margin compression. When INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average, S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days turn negative as investors anticipate earnings downgrades from reduced manufacturing activity and capacity utilization.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.3 on 2018-05-01 reflects manufacturing strength
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO falls below 6-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signal labor market momentum. When PAYEMS growth accelerates while UNRATE remains stable or declines, the combination indicates robust job creation without wage-driven inflation concerns, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors gain confidence in sustainable growth.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148,662K and UNRATE at 3.8% on 2018-05-01 show strong labor market
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or joint signal produces positive returns in < 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.5%, creating a negative real rate environment. This regime-specific mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the Federal Reserve is forced into tightening that compresses valuations and growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 250.535 and FEDFUNDS at 1.7% on 2018-05-01
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where real rates are negative, or Rank IC of interaction < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows deceleration below 2% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown. When GDPC1 growth falls below this threshold, S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days turn negative as investors anticipate recession risk and reduce equity allocations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,379.728B on 2018-01-01 reflects solid growth trajectory
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where GDPC1 growth is below 2%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline month-over-month signal deteriorating residential investment and forward demand weakness. When HOUST falls more than 5% month-over-month, S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days turn negative as investors interpret housing weakness as a leading indicator of broader economic deceleration.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,287K on 2018-04-01 shows resilient housing demand
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with housing market vulnerability
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST falls > 5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.
